# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1046
- **Lines of code**: 349,340
- **Languages**: TypeScript (879), CSS (44), JavaScript (39), Markdown (31), JSON (30)
- **Zones**: 36 (36 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-07T01:54:24.391Z

## Architecture Zones

### Architecture Docs (`architecture-docs`)

Design documents covering memory architecture, PRD stewardship, system evolution plans, and architectural reference material for the n-dx toolkit.

- Files: 16
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/architecture-docs/context.md`

### CLI End-to-End Tests (`cli-e2e-tests`)

Comprehensive end-to-end test suite validating all CLI commands, delegation paths, error handling, and architectural policy across the full monorepo.

- Files: 18
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-e2e-tests/context.md`

### Crash Recovery (`crash-recovery`)

Contains the crash detection utility and its companion recovery hook, forming the reliability layer that monitors viewer performance and initiates recovery on fatal errors.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.29, Coupling: 0.71
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/use-crash-recovery.ts, packages/web/src/viewer/performance/crash-detector.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/crash-recovery/context.md`

### Developer Analysis Scripts (`dev-analysis-scripts`)

One-off developer tooling scripts for call-graph analysis and zone consistency validation, not part of the production build pipeline.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/dev-analysis-scripts/context.md`

### Hench Agent (`hench-agent`)

The autonomous agent package responsible for selecting PRD tasks, running multi-turn tool-use loops against the LLM, and recording structured run history.

- Files: 160
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-agent/context.md`

Repository-level configuration, orchestration entry points, and project documentation co-located at the workspace root.

- Files: 27
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/monorepo-root/context.md`

Groups the interactive PRD editing UI components — analysis panel, batch import, proposal editor, and smart-add input — along with the analysis view that composes them.

- Files: 7

### PRD Runtime State (`prd-state`)

Rex runtime data directory containing the live PRD, execution logs, workflow records, and acknowledged findings that persist across agent runs.

- Files: 8
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/prd-state/context.md`

### Tick Timer (`tick-timer`)

Isolates the polling tick-timer utility and its tests, including a related elapsed-time memoization test that exercises timing behavior at the polling layer boundary.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Entry points: packages/web/src/viewer/polling/tick-timer.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/tick-timer/context.md`

Encapsulates task-level token usage tracking, incremental accumulation, and scheduled cleanup for the web server's analytics subsystem.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.75, Coupling: 0.25

### Web Dashboard (`web-dashboard`)

The primary web application zone containing the full viewer UI, server infrastructure, MCP endpoints, CLI entrypoint, and schema — the largest and most cohesive zone in the web package.

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

```
POST    /api/hench/workflow/apply
POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- E2E tests that spawn CLI subprocesses share no documented workspace isolation strategy; if tests run concurrently and both write to .rex/prd.json or .sourcevision/, results are non-deterministic. No test uses a dedicated temp directory per test file. [warning] (cli-e2e-tests)
- `_testHelpers` is exported through the production module surface of crash-detector.ts, bundling internal implementation details (storage keys, private functions) into the public API. Test-only exports should use a separate test-support file or conditional barrel to avoid polluting the production interface. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- hench-callgraph-analysis.mjs produces HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md but has no fail-fast guard that detects missing or stale input artifacts; silent success on a cold checkout produces a misleading (empty or stale) report with no error signal. [warning] (dev-analysis-scripts)
- The rex-gateway is imported directly by scattered consumer files across 160 files with no internal hench interface layer. When the rex gateway API changes, every call site must be updated individually with no intermediate abstraction to narrow the scope of change. An internal adapter or facade within hench that wraps the gateway would contain the blast radius. [warning] (hench-agent)
- The orchestration tier's architectural boundary (no direct package imports) is enforced solely by developer convention — no ESLint rule, TypeScript path mapping, or CI check prevents a direct `import` from cli.js into a domain package. A single accidental import would collapse the tier silently. [warning] (monorepo-root)
- claude-integration.js is a service file at the monorepo root that bypasses the gateway pattern entirely. Any cross-package imports it makes are invisible to gateway audits and import-graph coupling scores, creating an unmonitored coupling surface outside all four tiers of the dependency hierarchy. [warning] (monorepo-root)
- Proposal, ProposalFeature, and ProposalTask types are defined locally in analyze-panel.ts rather than imported from a shared rex schema or gateway. The local copy will silently diverge from the API response shape if the server-side PRD proposal format changes. [warning] (prd-analysis-panels)
- `useState(() => { loadPending(); })` in analyze-panel.ts misuses the useState initializer to trigger an async network call. This is not the intended use of the initializer (which sets initial synchronous state) and bypasses the standard effect lifecycle, making cleanup and double-invocation behavior undefined. [warning] (prd-analysis-panels)
- Web viewer unit tests (graph-interaction.test.ts, graph-zoom.test.ts) are excluded from packages/web per-package test coverage because they reside outside the package boundary in this zone; per-package coverage reports for the web package silently undercount viewer UI test coverage. [warning] (viewer-gateway-tests)
- Zone contains files from two physically distinct roots (packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ and tests/) with unrelated purposes; zone name 'viewer-gateway-tests' implies viewer scope only, actively misleading contributors about the gateway scripts' monorepo-wide scope. Should be split into two zones aligned to physical location and concern. [warning] (viewer-gateway-tests)
- ... and 2 more

### Patterns

- crash-recovery has only 1 incoming call-graph edge despite 3 cross-zone import edges from web-viewer. The zone is consumed by a single caller at runtime, making it a de facto singleton utility. This strengthens the case for absorbing it into web-dashboard as an internal sub-module rather than maintaining a separate zone boundary. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- Generated artifact HENCH_CALLGRAPH_FINDINGS.md is committed but has no CI regeneration-and-diff guard; it can silently go stale after hench source changes [warning] (dev-analysis-scripts)
- A single gateway file (src/prd/rex-gateway.ts) is the only cross-zone coupling surface for 160 files; gateway API breakage has maximum blast radius within hench — no incremental migration path exists if the rex API changes. [warning] (hench-agent)
- Orchestration-to-domain boundary is enforced at runtime only (subprocess spawning); a mismatched CLI argument or removed subcommand will produce a silent runtime failure with no compile-time safety net. [warning] (monorepo-root)
- prd-analysis-panels is a strict terminal node in the call graph (0 incoming, 3 outgoing). The bidirectional import relationship with web-dashboard noted in the zone-level cross-imports does not produce bidirectional call-graph edges — the reverse direction carries only type imports. No circular runtime dependency exists. [info] (prd-analysis-panels)
- tick-timer's 27:1 incoming-to-outgoing call ratio makes it the highest fan-in utility in the web package. Any change to its interface propagates to 27 call sites — it warrants a stable, explicitly versioned API contract and should never import from its consumers. [info] (tick-timer)
- usage-analytics-service has the cleanest architecture profile of any zone in this batch: one gateway, one consumer, zero transitive outbound calls. It is a textbook example of the gateway pattern applied correctly at zone granularity. [info] (usage-analytics-service)
- Zone conflates web-package unit tests with monorepo-root contract scripts; splitting into separate zones aligned to their physical location would improve discoverability and ownership clarity [warning] (viewer-gateway-tests)
- At a 6504:55 internal-to-outgoing call ratio, web-dashboard's outbound import edges are almost entirely type-only. Runtime coupling to the rest of the monorepo is effectively zero, which validates the gateway pattern at the call level, not just the import level. [info] (web-dashboard)
- web-viewer is the de facto internal API surface for the web-dashboard zone cluster: crash, panel, and web-unit all route through web-viewer rather than importing directly from web-dashboard. This hub-within-hub topology is architecturally sound but undocumented. [info] (web-dashboard)
- ... and 1 more

### Relationships

- Architectural rules documented here are enforced by tests in two separate zones with no automated link between them; the documentation and its enforcement tests can diverge without detection. [info] (architecture-docs)
- crash-recovery's 0 outgoing call-graph edges vs 3 outbound import edges to web-dashboard indicates the coupling is type-import-only. The zone's effective runtime isolation is much higher than its cohesion/coupling scores suggest — the metric overstates the structural risk. [info] (crash-recovery)
- tick-timer has zero coupling to web-dashboard at the call level (1 outgoing edge, none to web-dashboard) while web-dashboard has 4 inbound import edges from it — the dependency flows strictly upward from primitive to consumer, confirming a clean unidirectional layering that the zone-level bidirectional label obscures. [info] (tick-timer)
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 142 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (architecture-docs)
- Docs zone is fully isolated — no tooling enforces consistency between design docs and actual implementation, so decisions documented here may drift from code over time. [info] (architecture-docs)
- Level-system and steward-plan docs indicate planned structural evolution; these should be linked from the PRD so hench can track implementation progress. [info] (architecture-docs)
- Memory subsystem has at least five dedicated docs; consider consolidating into a stable reference document to reduce reader navigation overhead. [info] (architecture-docs)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- E2E tests spawn real CLI processes, so they are sensitive to build artifacts being stale; a pre-test build step or CI guard ensures test results reflect current source. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- The suite covers all major CLI surface areas (ci, config, delegation, dev, errors, init, plan, serve, sync, usage, work) providing strong regression coverage for the orchestration layer. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- architecture-policy.test.js and the gateway check scripts in viewer-gateway-tests both validate gateway rules — consider consolidating gateway policy assertions into one canonical location to avoid drift. [info] (cli-e2e-tests)
- High coupling (0.71) — 3 imports target "web-dashboard" [warning] (crash-recovery)
- Cohesion of 0.29 is below the warning threshold — the two files in this zone (hook and detector) are more coupled to web-dashboard than to each other, suggesting a zone boundary mismatch. [warning] (crash-recovery)
- ... and 38 more

### Suggestions

- packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/ contains files from 3 zones (crash-recovery, web-dashboard, web-package-scaffold) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Add a binary asset management convention to PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md covering: (1) where package logos/icons should live relative to the package root, (2) whether they must be excluded from npm publish via 'files' or .npmignore, and (3) whether the '-F' suffix for icon variants is an official convention. Both hench and web currently bundle PNG assets without documented intent. [info]
- Add a pnpm verify script (or Makefile target) that runs all three verification-tier mechanisms in sequence: vitest e2e, check-gateway-regex.mjs, check-gateway-test.mjs, and test-zone-consistency.mjs — removes the current requirement for contributors to manually discover and run four separate commands [warning]
- Add an intra-package layer-direction check to architecture-policy.test.js that parses import paths within each package and asserts domain files do not import from cli/ subdirectories — directly guards the existing packages-rex circular sub-zone violation and prevents recurrence [critical]
- Create a single gateway-rules.json (or equivalent) at the monorepo root that both check-gateway-regex.mjs and architecture-policy.test.js consume as the authoritative source of gateway file paths and allowed import patterns — eliminates silent divergence between two enforcement mechanisms [warning]
- Cross-zone test files (files that import from two or more production zones) should be placed in a dedicated tests/cross-zone/ or tests/integration/ directory rather than either zone's unit test folder. elapsed-time-memoization.test.ts is currently misclassified into tick-timer by the zone algorithm because its closest import is tick-timer.ts, despite also covering web-dashboard's elapsed-time.ts. A documented convention for cross-zone tests would prevent future misclassification. [info]
- Document the no-direct-peer-import invariant for web satellite zones (crash-recovery, tick-timer, prd-analysis-panels must not import from each other — only from web-viewer). Add a gateway policy rule enforcing this in the existing gateway check scripts. Currently the constraint exists empirically but has no enforcement mechanism, making it invisible to contributors adding new cross-zone imports. [info]
- PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md documents what gateways must do (re-export only, one per upstream package) but not where they must live. Hench places its gateway at src/prd/rex-gateway.ts; web places gateways at src/server/{rex,domain}-gateway.ts. Add a 'Gateway file placement' rule specifying the canonical directory and naming pattern to prevent future gateways from being created in ad-hoc locations. [info]
- Zone ID naming is inconsistent across the codebase: small/infrastructure zones use plain names (crash, panel, tests, scripts, e2e) while larger or web-specific zones use hyphenated compounds (web-viewer, hench-agent, web-server). Document a zone naming convention in sourcevision configuration or PACKAGE_GUIDELINES.md so that auto-detected zones and manually pinned zones follow a predictable scheme. [info]
- Hub function: walkTree in packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts is called from 29 files (high fan-in expected for this module type) — changes here have wide impact, consider if responsibilities can be narrowed [info]
- ... and 32 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
